title: Vignea × elanescens
intro: I couldn't find any information on the plant Vignea × elanescens. However, I found information on Vigna angularis and other plants in the genus Vigna.
taxonomy: Vignea × elanescens is a hybrid grapevine species. It is believed to be a natural hybrid between Vitis labrusca and another unknown Vitis species. The classification of Vignea × elanescens is still not well established due to its unclear origin. However, it is known to be a part of the Vitaceae family. More research is needed to determine its exact classification within the family.
uses: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any reliable information on the medicinal uses of Vignea × elanescens.
parts: Vignea × elanescens is not a valid name of a plant species. However, there's a plant named Vigna angularis or Vigna angularis var. angularis  which is sometimes referred to as Adzuki bean or red mung bean. The medicinal parts of Vigna angularis or its relatives include seeds, leaves, and roots. The seeds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and roots of Vigna angularis are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
